data.title: Gain Therapeutics: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on GT-02287 Clinical Momentum and Differentiated GCase Strategy; $7 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
Jason McCarthy, an analyst from Maxim Group, maintained the Buy rating on Gain Therapeutics. The associated price target remains the same with $7.00.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Jason McCarthy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including clinical momentum and a favorable competitive landscape for GT-02287 in Parkinson’s disease. The recent FDA clearance of the IND for a phase 2 PD study, along with upcoming nine‑month phase 1b data, supports continued development and underpins his confidence in Gain Therapeutics’ lead program.

He also views the failure of Bial’s GCase activator in phase 2 as reinforcing the differentiated potential of Gain’s GCase‑targeting chaperone approach. Because GT-02287 may exert broader cellular effects through protein folding and trafficking, and compares favorably to other GCase modulators such as Vanqua Bio’s VQ-101, McCarthy sees enhanced partnering prospects and long‑term value creation, justifying his unchanged $7 price target and Buy rating.

In another report released yesterday, Roth MKM also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $8.00 price target.

GANX’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from $3.220 to $1.990, which is a -38.20% drop .
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Revolution Medicines: Strong Zoldonrasib Data in G12D Pancreatic Cancer Support Buy Rating and Unchanged $263 Price Target
data.description:
In a report released today, Charles Zhu from LifeSci Capital maintained a Buy rating on Revolution Medicines, with a price target of $263.00.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Charles Zhu has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to the strength of zoldonrasib-based regimens in G12D-mutant pancreatic cancer. He views the updated efficacy data as meaningfully superior to prior standards, highlighting notably higher complete response rates and overall response rates for zoldonrasib combinations versus daraxon monotherapy or daraxon plus chemotherapy in comparable patient settings.

In addition, he interprets the maintained or improved dose intensity and the manageable safety profile as evidence that these regimens can be delivered at therapeutically robust exposures without unacceptable toxicity. Taken together, these results reinforce confidence in Revolution Medicines’ clinical strategy and support a favorable risk–reward profile for RVMD at the current valuation, justifying an unchanged $263 price target and a continued Buy recommendation.

In another report released on June 30, Truist Financial also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $210.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Jonathan Ho Maintains Buy on CrowdStrike, Citing Strong Execution, AI-Driven ARR Upside and Underpenetrated Cybersecurity Market
data.description:
William Blair analyst Jonathan Ho has maintained their bullish stance on CRWD stock, giving a Buy rating today.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Jonathan Ho has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, starting with CrowdStrike’s strong operational performance and clear ability to execute on its strategy. He views the company as well placed to expand its cybersecurity platform meaningfully, supported by rising demand and a large, still underpenetrated market opportunity.

Ho also points to the potential for faster annual recurring revenue growth as artificial intelligence tools roll out and as customers upgrade CCP deployments from trial to paid usage. While acknowledging competitive and innovation-related risks, he believes the company’s growth profile and cash-generation outlook justify a favorable valuation and continued Outperform stance.

data.title: Aritzia: Buy Rating Backed by Durable Growth Catalysts Despite Premium Valuation
data.description:
William Blair analyst Dylan Carden has maintained their bullish stance on ATZAF stock, giving a Buy rating today.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Dylan Carden has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including strong near-term operating momentum and structurally higher growth prospects. Recent intraquarter data point to sales exceeding expectations for the May quarter and an acceleration into the fiscal second quarter, underpinned by larger format stores, enhanced marketing channels, and a robust digital platform that together reinforce Aritzia’s merchandising strength.

At the same time, Carden recognizes that the current valuation already prices in a substantial portion of this upside, trading at a premium multiple versus prior cycles. Even so, he views today’s growth as being driven by more durable catalysts—such as expanding awareness, international growth, and a potential men’s opportunity via Reigning Champ—rather than a simple post-pandemic recovery. While a moderation in comparable sales could trigger near-term volatility around the upcoming investor day, he believes the company’s improved inventory discipline and accelerating square-footage expansion support sustained low- to mid-20% annualized growth, justifying a Buy rating despite the richer valuation.

According to TipRanks, Carden is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 8.5% and a 51.14% success rate. Carden covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Amazon, Chewy, and Boot Barn.

In another report released today, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a C$193.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
